page: Microsoft Azure HB176-48rs_v4 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the HBv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 CPU architecture. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU.

The instance includes 4332 GB of bundled local storage and is designated as Premium Storage capable. Additionally, the HB176-48rs_v4 supports Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) networking capabilities, which facilitates low-latency, high-throughput inter-node communication. No public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

With a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16:1 and substantial local storage, this instance offers high resource density for memory-bound and data-intensive workloads. The combination of dedicated vCPUs, RDMA capability, and large memory capacity makes this server type suitable for high-performance computing applications, large-scale simulations, in-memory databases, and complex data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure HB176-48rs_v4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the HBv4 family, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Designed for demanding workloads, it is RDMA-capable to support low-latency networking across nodes. This hardware profile provides high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive operations without requiring GPU acceleration. The server is configured for workloads such as large-scale scientific simulations, in-memory databases, and data-intensive engineering applications.
